title: Autoencoders & Bottleneck Architectures
summary: Encoder compresses input to a low-dimensional bottleneck, decoder reconstructs from the bottleneck. The bottleneck forces the model to learn a compact, meaningful representation. Used for: dimensionality reduction, anomaly detection (high reconstruction error = anomaly), denoising, feature learning.
key_insights: Architecture: Encoder → bottleneck (z) → Decoder. Loss = reconstruction error (MSE or BCE)
Information bottleneck: compression forces the model to learn what matters and discard noise
Anomaly detection: train on normal data only. Anomalies reconstruct poorly → high loss = anomaly signal
The bottleneck representation z IS the learned feature space — can be used for downstream tasks
Connects to transformer: masked autoencoder (MAE) uses transformer encoder with random masking

mathematical_core.encoder: z = f_enc(x) — maps input x ∈ ℝⁿ to bottleneck z ∈ ℝᵈ where d << n
mathematical_core.loss: L = ||x - x̂||² (MSE reconstruction) or BCE for binary data

related: TRANSFORMER.FFN — expand-then-compress is a per-token bottleneck

notes: Directly relevant to anomaly detection use case discussed during assessment. Also: self-attention for hypothesis discovery uses masked autoencoder training objective.
